top_signals.title: $70 Oil Could Put India Back on Track for 7% Economic Growth
top_signals.summary: India’s economy could return to the trajectory to grow by 7% and even more in the fiscal year through March 2027 if oil prices remain close to the current $70 per barrel, according to a senior official at India’s central bank. Oil prices at about $70 a barrel eased tensions in the Middle East, and a pick-up in tanker traffic at the Strait of Hormuz would reduce upward pressure on India’s inflation and improve the outlook for its economy, Nagesh Kumar, an external member of the Reserve Bank of India’s monetary policy committee,…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: AI IPO Wealth Seen Bolstering Luxury Industry Hurt by Iran War
top_signals.summary: The luxury sector is set to grow more slowly than previously predicted because of the Iran war, but wealth creation from the SpaceX listing and future US share sales by OpenAI and Anthropic could cushion the blow for struggling brands selling pricey handbags and watches, according to Bain & Co.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Gilt Returns Climb to Three-Month High After Oil-Fueled Recovery
top_signals.summary: Returns on UK government bonds are the highest in three months as tumbling oil prices offset investor concerns about unpredictable politics.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Bitcoin supply in loss reaches record high 10.83 million BTC
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.
